source.definition: to transition from being awake to sleeping

explanation.sourceText: The English phrase "fall asleep" combines the verb "fall" with the adjective "asleep" to describe the process of entering sleep. The verb "fall" comes from Old English "feallan", meaning to descend or drop, while "asleep" is derived from the Old English "on slæpe", meaning in sleep.
explanation.targetText: Expresia engleză „fall asleep“ combină verbul „fall“ cu adjectivul „asleep“ pentru a descrie procesul de a intra în somn. Verbul „fall“ provine din engleza veche „feallan“, care înseamnă a cădea sau a coborî, iar „asleep“ derivă din expresia engleză veche „on slæpe“, care înseamnă în somn.

source.additional: This phrase is commonly used in everyday English to describe the moment when someone begins to sleep.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a adormi" comes from the Latin "dormire", meaning "to sleep". The prefix "a-" marks the verb and indicates the transition into the state of sleeping.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a adormi“ provine din latinescul „dormire“, care înseamnă „a dormi“. Prefixul „a-“ marchează verbul și indică trecerea în starea de somn.

target.additional: Verb of the 4th conjugation, frequently used to describe the beginning of sleep, e.g., „a adormi rapid“ (to fall asleep quickly).

examples.sourceText: I always fall asleep at ten.

learningTips.text: Remember the phrase "fall asleep" by imagining yourself gently falling into a soft bed, transitioning smoothly from wakefulness to sleep.
The Romanian word "adormi" sounds like "a dorm" — think of a dormitory where people go to sleep, helping you link the word to sleeping.
Visualize the prefix "a-" in Romanian as 'about to' or 'starting to', so "a adormi" means 'to start sleeping'.
Connect "adormi" with the English word "dormant" which means inactive or asleep, to remember the meaning of the Romanian verb.
